summary: Matched the fighter common DamageIce function ftCo_80091854 and improved, but did not fully match, DamageFall helper ftCo_80090594. The PR implemented previously NOT_IMPLEMENTED DamageIce collision/bounce logic around ftCo_800914A4 and ftCo_80091620, corrected DamageIce jump setup in ftCo_80091854, refined DamageFall model shift logic, and added small type/header/prototype fixes needed by the match. The decomp.dev bot reported one new full match, ftCo_80091854, and an improvement for ftCo_80090594 from 47.62% to 58.73%. Human review evidence is sparse; the author explicitly asked for help on the remaining mismatch in ftCo_80090594.
what_changed: Implemented substantial DamageIce behavior in src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_DamageIce.c, including ftCo_800914A4 collision handling and ftCo_80091620 ice-impact/effect/bounce logic, replacing NOT_IMPLEMENTED stubs.

Matched ftCo_80091854 according to the decomp.dev report, including using GET_FIGHTER, clearing fp->x2227_b6, changing motion state to 0x146, spawning effect 1091 with XRotN joint, zero vector constant ftCo_803B74BC, and scale-derived parameter.

Improved ftCo_80090594 in ftCo_DamageFall.c by introducing calcShift(hitlag_mul, msid, dmg), using ftCommon_CalcHitlag through p_ftCommonData, removing redundant fp2/fCom temporaries, and assigning x18FD from Fighter_804D6530 indexing.
Added includes in DamageIce for camera, DamageFall, and Kirby init dependencies used by the newly implemented logic.
Refined ftCommonData fields x780 and x784 from UNK_T to float, matching their use as velocity magnitude threshold and velocity multiplier in ftCo_80091620.
Changed ftCommon_MotionVars damageice.x0 from s8 to u8, aligning the field with state values 1, 2, and 3 used for wall/ceiling collision tracking.
Adjusted C prototypes and formatting in unrelated or incidental files: gm_801721EC_2(void), gm_801721EC_4(void), it_802CB8A4(void), include ordering/spacing, and brace formatting.
smart_moves: Used a small helper, calcShift, to isolate the DamageFall model-shift calculation and force the calculation through ftCommon_CalcHitlag(dmg, msid, hitlag_mul), improving codegen for ftCo_80090594.
Converted unknown common-data fields to float only where the diff shows direct floating-point use, giving the compiler better type information for DamageIce velocity comparisons and scaling.

Preserved matching-sensitive structure with explicit temporaries, stack padding, and assignment patterns in DamageIce, including PAD_STACK and named locals for rand, range, effect_joint, and bubble_ratio.
Used a local inline my_sqrtf built around __frsqrte and repeated Newton refinement, a common PowerPC matching tactic for sqrt-like codegen.
Replaced manual component copies with direct Vec3 struct assignment where it matched, such as sp20 = ftCo_803B74B0 and vec = ftCo_803B74BC.
Threaded effect spawning through existing fighter effect handles, joints, and parameters instead of placeholder calls, making ftCo_80091854 fully match per bot output.
decomp_lessons: When a field participates in floating-point comparisons or multipliers, changing it from UNK_T to float can be necessary for both correctness and instruction selection.
Signedness of motion-variable bytes matters: damageice.x0 needed to be u8 rather than s8 for the implemented state checks.
For fighter code, GET_FIGHTER(gobj) can produce better matching code than directly reading gobj->user_data in some contexts.
Extracting a matching-sensitive arithmetic expression into a static helper can alter register pressure and instruction ordering enough to improve an unmatched function.
Effect-spawn calls often require the exact argument shape, including taking addresses of fighter effect fields and stack locals; missing varargs-style parameters can prevent matching.
Collision response code may require preserving explicit wall/ceiling env flag masks and separate left/right/top correction vectors rather than abstracting the cases too aggressively.
Do not overstate completion from a broad PR title: ftCo_80091854 matched, but ftCo_80090594 remained only improved, and the author explicitly said they were stumped on that remaining mismatch.
naming_conventions: Existing decomp symbol style uses address-based names such as ftCo_80091854, ftCo_80090594, ftCo_800914A4, and ftCo_80091620 until semantic names are known.
Fighter common motion-state constants use ftCo_MS_* names, shown by ftCo_MS_DamageIce.
Ground/air enum values use GA_* names, shown by GA_Air.
Fighter part names use FtPart_* names, shown by FtPart_XRotN, FtPart_TransN, and FtPart_TopN.
The PR still introduced placeholder names SOME_CONSTANT_IDK and SOME_CONSTANT_IDK_2, which are evidence of unresolved semantic naming and should be revisited when constants are identified.
Empty-parameter C functions were normalized to explicit void prototypes in gm_16F1.c and itmatadogas.c.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Added PAD_STACK in ftCo_800914A4 and ftCo_80091854 to influence stack layout.
Used volatile float in my_sqrtf to shape floating-point codegen around __frsqrte.
Used repeated Newton-Raphson refinement constants .5 and 3.0 in my_sqrtf for PowerPC reciprocal-square-root matching.
Used explicit address casts such as *(float*) &fp->co_attrs.x144 in ftCo_80091854, indicating a codegen/type workaround for a field access.
Used bit/boolean field corrections such as fp->x2227_b6 and fp->x2219_b0, which can be matching-sensitive in packed fighter flags.
Assigned fp->dmg.x18FD with a casted indexed lookup from Fighter_804D6530: (u8) (u32) Fighter_804D6530[fp->dmg.x18F8 * 2 + 1].
Kept explicit env_flags masks 0x800, 0x20, and 0x4000 in DamageIce collision handling.
review_feedback: Author note: ftCo_80090594 was not fully matched; the author linked a decomp.me scratch and asked what was missing.
decomp.dev bot reported matched code increased by +308 bytes and +0.01%.
decomp.dev bot reported one new match: main/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_DamageIce ftCo_80091854, from 58.44% to 100.00%, +129 bytes.
decomp.dev bot reported one unmatched-function improvement: main/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_DamageFall ftCo_80090594, from 47.62% to 58.73%, +28 bytes.
No human review comments or formal review records were present in the provided slice.

Find later PRs that fully match ftCo_80090594 or further modify calcShift in ftCo_DamageFall.c.
